"Thanks to ŌĆŗthe good cooperation with Ukraine, we are ŌĆīlearning ŌüĀdirectly from this. This also offers opportunities for our business community," she added. The ŌĆŗdrones will ŌĆŗbe ŌüĀmanufactured in the Netherlands and Ukraine.
NATO allies have ŌĆŗalready provided over $4.5 billion ŌĆŗin ŌüĀmilitary equipment for Ukraine, according to data from the U.S. ŌüĀambassador ŌĆŗto NATO Matthew ŌĆŗWhitaker released in February.
($1 = 0.8475 euros)

item.title: Europe sees record jet inflows from US in April
item.description:
LONDON, April 15 (Reuters) - Europe was seeing record inflows of jet fuel from the United States, data from Kpler and LSEG showed, as the region attempts to shore up fuel supplies to make up for the disruption ŌĆŗof imports from the Middle East.
The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz due ŌĆŗto the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has blocked flows from the Middle East, ŌüĀon which Europe depends on for nearly 75% of its jet fuel imports, or around 375,000 ŌĆŗbarrels per day. This has led European airlines to urge the European Union to step in ŌĆŗwith emergency measures, including widespread airspace closures, as per a document seen by Reuters.
Inflows from the U.S. are due to reach between 149,000 to 200,000 barrels per day so far in April, based on vessels discharged and those still ŌĆŗdue to, which is already at record highs per data going as far back as 2015 ŌĆŗon LSEG and 2017 on Kpler.
Despite the requirement in the EU countries to maintain 90 days of emergency ŌĆīoil reserves, ŌüĀthis doesn't stipulate how much of it should be a specific fuel. Per the International Energy Agency , Spain is a net exporter of jet fuel, while Britain, which is also the regionŌĆÖs largest consumer, imports 65% of its demand. Stocks of jet fuel held independently in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp refining and ŌĆŗstorage hub stood at ŌĆŗtheir lowest since March ŌüĀ2023 last week.
The U.S. is, however, already exporting at record highs - in the week ending April 3, the country exported an estimated 442,000 barrels of ŌĆŗjet fuel in total, double the 219,000 barrels averaged last year per ŌĆŗdata from ŌüĀthe Energy Information Administration.
While the U.S. is the top consumer of jet fuel globally, exports to regions with a worse fuel crunch like Europe and Asia were fetching better prices.
The IEA also added in its ŌüĀmonthly ŌĆŗreport that if European markets are unable to secure more ŌĆŗthan 50% of volumes lost from the Middle East, then stocks would hit the crucial 23-day stockpile level in June - ŌĆŗthe level at which physical shortages would begin.

item.title: US Treasury chief says he expects gasoline to be in $3 dollar range this summer
item.description: WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - Treasury Secretary ŌĆŗScott Bessent ŌĆīon Wednesday told ŌĆŗreporters ŌĆŗhe has been meeting ŌüĀwith ŌĆŗMiddle East ŌĆŗcounterparts about the Iran oil ŌĆŗcrisis ŌĆŗand that he ŌĆīexpects ŌüĀU.S. consumers will see gasoline ŌĆŗprices ŌĆŗdrop ŌüĀto the $3 ŌĆŗrange this ŌĆŗsummer.

item.title: Russia delivers second post-sanctions LNG cargo from Portovaya to China, data shows
item.description:
MOSCOW, April 15 (Reuters) - Russian energy giant Gazprom (GAZP.MM) , opens new tab has delivered a second post-sanctions LNG cargo from the Baltic ŌĆŗSea's Portovaya plant to China, LSEG data ŌĆīshowed on Wednesday.
The small-scale Portovaya plant, which has a production capacity of 1.5 million tons of LNG per ŌĆŗyear, started operations in September 2022.
Exports from the ŌĆŗplant have been interrupted by additional U.S. sanctions , opens new tab over ŌüĀthe Ukraine war that were imposed in January ŌĆŗ2025 to disrupt Russia's ability to produce and ŌĆŗexport LNG and reduce its revenue from the trade.
Gas carrier Valera, formerly known as Velikiy Novgorod, loaded the cargo at the ŌĆŗBaltic Sea plant on January 25 and ŌĆŗdelivered it to the Beihai LNG terminal on Wednesday, LSEG ŌĆīship-tracking ŌüĀdata showed.
The first cargo from the plant after a hiatus was delivered to the terminal last December.
Another of Russia's sanctioned plants, Arctic LNG-2, also supplies LNG to Beihai.
Before ŌĆŗthe sanctions, ŌĆŗPortovaya shipped ŌüĀtwo cargoes a month on average during winter. Since March 2025, beyond the ŌĆŗtwo shipments to China, it has ŌĆŗbeen sending ŌüĀone cargo per month to the Russian western exclave of Kaliningrad.
In the early stages of its ŌüĀoperations, ŌĆŗmost cargoes from Portovaya were delivered ŌĆŗto Turkey and Greece with markets subsequently widening to China, ŌĆŗSpain and Italy.

item.title: Earthquake of magnitude 5.93 strikes off coast of Costa Rica, GFZ says
item.description:
April 15 (Reuters) - An earthquake ŌĆŗof magnitude ŌĆī5.93 struck off ŌĆŗthe coast ŌĆŗof Costa Rica ŌüĀearly ŌĆŗon Wednesday, ŌĆŗthe German Research Centre ŌĆŗfor ŌĆŗGeosciences said.
The quake ŌĆīhit ŌüĀat 0656 GMT at a ŌĆŗdepth ŌĆŗof ŌüĀ18 km (11.18 ŌĆŗmiles), ŌĆŗGFZ ŌüĀsaid.

item.title: Israeli military continues to strike Hezbollah amid Lebanon peace talks, Netanyahu says
item.description:
JERUSALEM, April 15 (Reuters) - Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Wednesday the Israeli military continued to strike at Hezbollah and ŌĆŗwas about to overcome what he described as the militia's ŌĆīstronghold of Bint Jbeil, as pressure mounted for a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.
In a video statement, Netanyahu said he has instructed the military ŌĆŗto continue reinforcing the security zone in southern Lebanon ŌĆŗwhile at the same time negotiating a peace ŌüĀdeal with Beirut.
"These negotiations have not taken place for over ŌĆŗ40 years. They are happening now because we are very ŌĆŗstrong, and countries are coming to us - not only Lebanon," he said.
He said that in talks with Lebanon, Israel has two main objectives - dismantling ŌĆŗof Hezbollah and a sustainable peace that is "achieved through ŌĆŗstrength."
Conflict between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon reignited after U.S.-Israeli strikes ŌĆīon ŌüĀIran on February 28 triggered a wider conflict in the Middle East.
Netanyahu said Israeli forces were focused on Bint Jbeil, which he called the capital of Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.
"We are, in ŌĆŗeffect, about ŌĆŗto eliminate this ŌüĀgreat stronghold of Hezbollah," he said.
On Iran, Netanyahu said the U.S. keeps Israel updated and ŌĆŗthe two countries are aligned on their goals ŌĆŗto ŌüĀsee enriched nuclear material removed from Iran, the cancellation of enrichment capabilities within Iran, and the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
"It is too ŌüĀearly ŌĆŗto say how this matter will ŌĆŗend, or even how it will progress," he said. Should fighting resume, "we are ŌĆŗprepared for any scenario."

item.title: White House denies U.S. requested ceasefire, says new talks may happen in Pakistan
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - Reports that the White House has requested a ceasefire in the Iran ŌĆŗwar are wrong, press secretary Karoline Leavitt told ŌĆīreporters on Wednesday but added that discussions about a second round of talks with the Iranians were ongoing and productive.
Speaking at a White ŌĆŗHouse press briefing, Leavitt said any fresh talks ŌĆŗwould likely be in Pakistan again as it ŌüĀhas emerged as the "only mediator" in the effort to ŌĆŗend the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.
"These conversations are productive and ŌĆŗongoing, and that's where we are right now. I've also seen some reporting about the potentiality for in-person discussions. Again, those discussions ŌĆŗare being had, but nothing is official until you ŌĆŗhear it from us here at the White House, but we feel ŌĆīgood ŌüĀabout the prospects of a deal," Leavitt said.
The talks last weekend broke down without an agreement to end the war, which President Donald Trump began alongside Israel on ŌĆŗFebruary 28, triggering ŌĆŗIranian attacks ŌüĀon Iran's Gulf neighbors and reigniting a conflict between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in ŌĆŗLebanon.
The war has led Iran to effectively shut ŌĆŗthe ŌüĀStrait of Hormuz - a vital artery for global crude and gas shipments - to ships other than its own, sharply reducing ŌüĀexports ŌĆŗfrom the Gulf, particularly to Asia ŌĆŗand Europe, and leaving energy importers scrambling for alternative supplies.

item.title: US Senate Republicans block bid to rein in Trump Iran war powers, voting continues
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - A majority of ŌĆŗthe U.S. ŌĆīSenate backed President Donald Trump's ŌĆŗmilitary campaign ŌĆŗagainst Iran on Wednesday, ŌüĀvoting to ŌĆŗblock a ŌĆŗDemocratic-led resolution aiming to stop the ŌĆŗwar until ŌĆŗhostilities are authorized by ŌĆīCongress.
As ŌüĀvoting continued, the tally was 51-47 to ŌĆŗblock ŌĆŗthe ŌüĀwar powers resolution, as ŌĆŗalmost ŌĆŗall ŌüĀof Trump's fellow Republicans voted ŌüĀagainst ŌĆŗit.

item.title: Israeli attacks prevent Lebanese from burying their dead in ancestral lands
item.description:
BEIRUT April 15 - Brothers Ali and Wael ŌĆŗSabbagh stood facing the remains of the building that contained their family home for more than 50 years.
The ŌĆīsite was pounded into rubble by Israeli strikes last week in an attack that killed their mother, brother, and the family's helper.
Their grief has been compounded by their inability to properly bury their mother and brother ŌĆö a plight shared by other families from south Lebanon. Unable to head to their ancestral lands to ŌĆŗhonor their dead, they are forced to bury them temporarily in Beirut until it becomes safe enough to allow their ŌĆŗremains to be moved to their final resting place.
"We come from the south of Lebanon, from Nabatieh, and ŌüĀthat is being bombarded on a daily basis," Wael said. "We want them to be buried in the family cemetery, but unfortunately we're ŌĆŗunable to do that."
Israel has vowed to establish a buffer zone in southern Lebanon and maintain control over territory ŌĆŗup to about 30 km (20 miles) north of Israel's border.
Nabatieh, a major city in the south, has been mostly reduced to rubble. The brothers said they cannot risk the journey south, nor can they send someone in their place for fear of them being killed in the strikes.
"Nobody should have to go ŌĆŗthrough this. And unfortunately, my brother, sister, and I are not the only ones going through this," Wael said. "Hundreds, if not thousands, of ŌĆŗfamilies...are mourning in the same way I am, not understanding what happened."
The Israeli attacks on Beirut last week were some of the most violent strikes on ŌĆīthe Lebanese ŌüĀcapital in decades. Across the country, more than 350 people were killed, said Lebanese authorities.
More than 2,160 people have been killed in Lebanon by Israeli attacks since the war between Hezbollah and Israel erupted last month. Hezbollah had lobbed rockets at Israel, prompting Israel to retaliate and launch an all-out war on Lebanon. More than 1.2 million people have been displaced in Lebanon since, said Lebanese authorities.
The Israeli military has said its ŌĆŗoperations in Lebanon target Hezbollah.
Ali and ŌĆŗWael's mother's body was found ŌüĀin her home. It took two days for the family to track down their brother, whom they were able to identify by his arm tattoo. The family made arrangements to repatriate the body of ŌĆŗthe house helper to Ethiopia.
The brothers spoke to Reuters a day after Lebanese and Israeli officials ŌĆŗmet in Washington in ŌüĀa rare encounter between representatives of governments that have technically been in a state of war since Israel was established in 1948.
"We're always going to be under the foot of the Israelis," Wael said. The brothers said they will try to seek legal action against the people behind the ŌüĀkilling of ŌĆŗtheir family members.
Ali lamented the cycle of death, destruction and resurgence that has ŌĆŗmarked the lives of the Lebanese for most of their country's existence.
Already, behind the Sabbaghs' bombed-out home, construction workers were busy working on a new building.
"Come back in a few years, ŌĆŗthere's a new building, new kids, new mothers, new brothers," Ali said. "I will come. I will remember. Nobody else will."

item.title: Russia warns drone plans for Ukraine are pulling Europe deeper into war
item.description:
MOSCOW, April 15 (Reuters) - Russia's defence ministry warned on Wednesday that European ŌĆŗplans to step up drone supplies to Ukraine are dragging ŌĆīthose countries deeper into a war with Russia.
The ministry said it believes governments in a number of EU countries have decided to increase ŌĆŗthe production and supply of drones to Ukraine, a ŌĆŗmove Moscow views as a step that is escalating ŌüĀthe conflict.
It published a list of factories and enterprises in ŌĆŗseveral European countries it alleges manufacture drones or drone components, ŌĆŗand gave their addresses, including sites in Britain, Germany, Spain, Italy, Israel and Poland, among others.
"The European public should not only have a clear ŌĆŗunderstanding of the true causes of the threats to their ŌĆŗsecurity, but also be aware of the addresses and locations of Ukrainian and ŌĆījoint ŌüĀenterprises producing UAVs and components for Ukraine within their own countries," it said.
Former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev, now deputy chair of Russia's Security Council, said in a subsequent post on X ŌĆŗthat the list ŌĆŗpublished by ŌüĀthe military amounted to a list of potential targets for Russia's armed forces.
"When strikes become a ŌĆŗreality depends on what comes next. Sleep well, ŌĆŗEuropean ŌüĀpartners!" he said.
Russian officials, including Medvedev, have repeatedly made public statements that have been perceived as threats to European countries over their ŌüĀsupport for ŌĆŗUkraine. However, these have generally taken ŌĆŗthe form of warnings and veiled hints rather than outright announcements of imminent ŌĆŗstrikes.

item.title: Colombia court orders Petro government to return emergency funds
item.description:
BOGOTA, April 15 (Reuters) - Colombia's Constitutional Court ordered President Gustavo Petro's government on Wednesday to return funds collected during an ŌĆŗeconomic emergency that was declared unconstitutional.
The decision is the latest blow ŌĆīto the Petro administration's finances, strained by setbacks in Congress, where several proposals for fiscal reforms have failed to gain support.
The court had struck down the economic emergency declared ŌĆŗby Petro's government in January to raise 11 trillion pesos ($3.07 billion) ŌĆŗto finance part of this year's budget.
The government had collected 1.67 ŌüĀtrillion pesos ($467 million) as a result of the decrees enacted during the ŌĆŗeconomic emergency, according to a source at the country's National Tax and Customs ŌĆŗDirectorate who spoke on condition of anonymity.
The economic emergency is a state of exception that allows the government to make decisions by decree without prior authorization from Congress.
Among the measures ŌĆŗthe government had established during the state of emergency were a tax ŌĆŗon liquid assets exceeding 2.1 billion pesos, a special income tax for the financial sector, ŌĆīand ŌüĀa 19% tax on the sale of alcoholic beverages and gambling, as well as on certain vehicles, motorcycles, and aircraft, among others.
On previous occasions when the court declared other economic emergencies unconstitutional, the ruling was not retroactive; meaning it ŌĆŗdid not require the ŌĆŗgovernment to return ŌüĀthe funds already collected.
Latin AmericaŌĆÖs fourth-largest economy is experiencing a deterioration in its finances, which forced the government in ŌĆŗJune to suspend a fiscal ruling under which the Ministry ŌĆŗof ŌüĀFinance raised the fiscal deficit target for 2025 to 7.1% of GDP, from an original 5.1%.
Last week, Finance Minister German Avila announced that he would present a new ŌüĀtax reform ŌĆŗbill to Congress worth 16 trillion pesos ($4.47 ŌĆŗbillion) to finance this yearŌĆÖs spending budget, less than three months before the end of the legislative ŌĆŗperiod.
($1= 3,578.82 pesos)

item.title: Sovereign borrowers launch platform to amplify voice in debt talks
item.description:
April 15 (Reuters) - A group of officials from developing economies launched on Wednesday ŌĆīthe Borrowers' Platform, an effort backed by the United Nations to give debt-hit nations a stronger collective voice in dealings with creditors.
United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said at the launch event that the platform is essential for power relations to change.
"Today, ŌĆŗwe launch a breakthrough in global financing, a platform in which borrowing countries sit together, ŌĆŗlearn from each other and speak with a collective voice," Guterres said.
He said 3.4 ŌüĀbillion people live in countries that spend more on debt service than on health or education.
The ŌĆŗinitiative is seen partly as a counter for institutions like the Paris Club, where official creditors meet to ŌĆŗdiscuss negotiation strategies when a common debtor encounters problems with repayment.
This platform aims also to be an experience repository of sorts, where members share their knowledge and new governments, or new countries, avoid arriving at the table with a ŌĆŗlack of preparedness.
"What was once a long-standing aspiration of developing countries has now become a concrete and a ŌĆŗcollective step forward," said Ahmed Kouchouk, Egypt's finance minister. "Today stands as a strong statement of intent that the voice of borrowing ŌĆīnations ŌüĀand countries belongs at the very center of the global financial dialogue."
The event, presided over by Egypt as chair of the working group, is due to formally open an interim phase for the platform, establish interim leadership and adopt a work program running through October 2026, according to the launch agenda.
The ŌĆŗworking group that developed the ŌĆŗdraft modalities was ŌüĀmade up of Egypt, Colombia, Honduras, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan and Zambia. Pakistan served as vice chair.
Under the draft framework, full membership would be voluntary and limited ŌĆŗto developing countries that are UN member states, net borrowers and not ŌĆŗfull members of ŌüĀcreditor groupings. The proposed structure includes a Governing Council of finance ministers, central bank governors or equivalent officials, and a Steering Committee of senior technical officials.
"The launch of the Borrowers' Platform is a major milestone ŌüĀin rebalancing ŌĆŗpower inequalities in global economic governance," said Iolanda Fresnillo, policy and ŌĆŗadvocacy manager at the European Network on Debt and Development (Eurodad), in a statement.
"This long-overdue initiative is a first step towards breaking creditor domination over decision-making ŌĆŗon sovereign debt issues."

item.title: US targets Iran's oil transportation infrastructure with sanctions
item.description:
April 15 (Reuters) - The U.S. Treasury Department said ŌĆŗon Wednesday it was targeting Iran's ŌĆīoil transportation infrastructure by imposing sanctions on more than two dozen individuals, companies, and ŌĆŗvessels.
The sanctions target a network of ŌĆŗIranian oil shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein ŌüĀShamkhani, the Treasury Department said in ŌĆŗa statement. Hossein Shamkhani is the son ŌĆŗof Ali Shamkhani, a key figure in Iran's security and nuclear policymaking who was killed in U.S.-Israeli ŌĆŗstrikes on Tehran on February 28.
"Treasury ŌĆŗis moving aggressively with Economic Fury by targeting ŌĆīregime ŌüĀelites like the Shamkhani family that attempt to profit at the expense of the Iranian people," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent ŌĆŗsaid in ŌĆŗthe ŌüĀstatement.
The Treasury Department is also sanctioning Iranian national Seyed Naiemaei ŌĆŗBadroddin Moosavi, who it alleges to ŌĆŗbe ŌüĀa financier to Hezbollah, and three companies linked to a money laundering scheme ŌüĀinvolving ŌĆŗthe sale of Iranian ŌĆŗoil in exchange for Venezuelan gold.

item.title: Allbirds lacks footing for dramatic AI pivot
item.description:
NEW YORK, April 15 (Reuters Breakingviews) - After failing to sustain its eco-friendly sneaker empire, Allbirds (BIRD.O) , opens new tab has an even woolier proposition. The company, once valued at ŌĆŗmore than $4 billion, sold , opens new tab what remains of its footwear business last month for just $39 million, and is ŌĆīnow racing into , opens new tab the world of artificial intelligence. These sorts of bizarre pivots work occasionally, but the odds are long.
Some modern corporate giants have sprouted from highly unusual seeds. South Korean smartphone and chip maker Samsung once exported dried fish while Nokia made the unlikely ŌĆŗswitch from rubber-boot manufacturing to wireless telecom. India's Wipro started by selling cooking oil before becoming an ŌĆŗIT powerhouse. Shell is so named because of its origins as a collectibles shop where ŌüĀfounder Marcus Samuel once sold , opens new tab decorative sea-creature coverings before it later commissioned the first oil tanker to traverse the ŌĆŗSuez Canal.
Most such ambitious metamorphoses flop, however. They suffer from a lack of management skill, capital, luck, competition and myriad ŌĆŗother factors. After all, defeat is the eventual fate for most ventures, and the bigger the gambit the lower the chance for success.
Jumping onto the latest bandwagon, as Allbirds is doing, also tends to be little more than a gimmick, or worse. The Long ŌĆŗIsland Iced Tea Corp, for example, attracted investors in 2021 after it rebranded as Long Blockchain to take advantage ŌĆŗof cryptocurrency mania. Its shares were later delisted, and the Securities and Exchange Commission brought insider trading charges , opens new tab .
In addition to raising $50 million ŌĆīin convertible ŌüĀfinancing, Allbirds is playing the name game, too. It will now be known as NewBird AI, with a ŌĆ£long term vision to become a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service and AI-native cloud solutions provider.ŌĆØ The microscopic stock price jumped 620% on Wednesday.
ItŌĆÖs unclear what the company brings to the crowded and heavily capitalized world of data centers. CoreWeave (CRWV.O) , opens new tab , for one, is ŌĆŗworth 400 times as much, ŌĆŗhas close ties with ŌüĀNvidia and just expanded chunky deals with Jane Street and Anthropic. Despite this progress, it is not clear what advantage it has over deep-pocketed and technologically advantaged rivals such ŌĆŗas Microsoft (MSFT.O) , opens new tab and Amazon (AMZN.O) , opens new tab .
In this context, NewBird AI looks like a considerable longshot. Its ŌĆŗshoes may have ŌüĀspread across Silicon Valley, but it will take more than a few dollars and a dream to get off the backfoot.
Follow Robert Cyran on Bluesky , opens new tab .

item.title: Exclusive: European buyers hold talks to ship Canadian LNG via Panama Canal to diversify supply
item.description:
CALGARY, April 15 (Reuters) - European buyers, including Germany's Uniper, are exploring the possibility of purchasing liquefied ŌĆŗnatural gas from Canada's Pacific coast and shipping it through the Panama Canal as part of a long-term strategy to diversify supply, ŌĆīan effort made more urgent in the wake of the Iran war, two sources familiar with the matter said.
Three sources said European buyers are among the potential customers that have had commercial talks with Canada's Ksi Lisims LNG , a proposed liquefied natural gas export terminal whose backers have been working to finalize contracts with purchasers before making an expected final investment ŌĆŗdecision this year.
The interest in Ksi Lisims from potential European customers ŌĆö who include state-owned German energy group Uniper (UN0k.DE) , opens new tab , according to two sources ŌĆö ŌĆŗis significant, as Canada's burgeoning LNG export industry is geographically more suited to supplying Asia and tolls and shipping time ŌüĀthrough the Panama Canal will increase costs.
All of Canada's existing and emerging LNG export capacity is located on the country's west coast, offering short ŌĆŗshipping times to Asian buyers, while its east coast has virtually no infrastructure beyond Repsol's < REP.MC , opens new tab > Saint John terminal.
Lack of infrastructure has long been seen as a ŌĆŗbarrier to Canada supplying any meaningful amounts of LNG to Europe. But a source familiar with the Ksi Lisims LNG project said the current Middle East conflict means European buyers are considering accepting the costs and longer transit time of shipping through the Panama Canal in order to diversify their supply sources to a stable, democratic ŌĆŗjurisdiction like Canada.
"Since the war in Iran started, there has been especially strong interest in (Ksi Lisims) offtake from LNG buyers all around the world, ŌĆŗincluding from Europe," the source said.
Uniper declined to comment. Reuters reported in March that the German company was in talks with Canada at a corporate and political level to ŌĆīexpand its ŌüĀliquefied natural gas purchases. The United States accounted for 96% of Germany's LNG imports last year, and two sources said Ksi Lisims is seen having potential when it comes to diversifying that supply.
The Ksi Lisims project is not an immediate solution to Europe's energy needs. Even if its proponents ŌĆö Houston-based Western LNG; a consortium of Canadian natural gas producers called Rockies LNG; and the Nisga'a First Nation, who own the land for the project ŌĆö decide ŌĆŗto go ahead, it will be ŌĆŗin construction for several years.
However, ŌüĀthe project has been referred by the Canadian government to Canada's major projects office for fast-tracking. Prime Minister Mark Carney has sought to speed up natural resource project permitting to boost an economy that has been threatened by U.S. trade ŌĆŗpolicy.
< SHEL.L , opens new tab > and < TTEF.PA , opens new tab > have already signed 20-year LNG purchase agreements with Ksi Lisims.
Canada's Energy and Natural Resources Minister said ŌĆŗin August German companies are ŌüĀlooking to buy and swap Canadian LNG cargoes. But now, they are also interested in taking shipments of the physical Canadian product, the sources said.
The Iran war and the resulting Strait of Hormuz closure have made it increasingly likely that Ksi Lisims gets built, said Jamie Heard, vice president, capital markets for Tourmaline ŌüĀOil (TOU.TO) , opens new tab , one ŌĆŗof the Rockies LNG partners behind the project.
He said Western Canadian natural gas prices continue ŌĆŗto lag behind the U.S. benchmark, making a strong business case for Canadian LNG.
"The economic rationale is there, these projects can pay themselves off quite quickly with spreads at that level," ŌĆŗHeard said in an interview.

item.title: Bessent says U.S. won't be renewing waivers for Iranian and Russian oil
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - The United States will not be renewing ŌĆŗthe waivers that allowed purchase of some Iranian and ŌĆīRussian oil without facing U.S. sanctions, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told reporters on Wednesday.
Reuters reported on Tuesday that ŌĆŗWashington would not renew a 30-day waiver of ŌĆŗsanctions on Iranian oil at sea that ŌüĀexpires this week, and let a similar waiver ŌĆŗon sanctions on Russian oil expire over the weekend.
"We ŌĆŗwill not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license ŌĆŗon Iranian oil. That was oil that ŌĆŗwas on the water prior to March 11. So all ŌĆīthat ŌüĀhas been used," Bessent said at a White House briefing.
The moves signal an end to the Trump administration's efforts to use the sanctions waivers ŌĆŗto free ŌĆŗup more ŌüĀoil supplies and lower soaring global energy prices.
The Iranian waiver, which the ŌĆŗTreasury Department issued on March 20, allowed ŌĆŗsome ŌüĀ140 million barrels of oil to reach global markets and helped relieve pressure on energy supply ŌüĀduring ŌĆŗthe war, Bessent said last ŌĆŗmonth. The waiver is set to expire on April 19.

item.title: Higher oil output gives Nigeria fiscal space as reforms continue ŌĆō Edun
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - Nigeria's oil production ŌĆŗhas risen to 1.8 million barrels a day, Finance Minister Wale Edun told Reuters, generating fiscal breathing ŌĆīspace that will allow the government to support vulnerable households as it ploughs ahead with reforms.
Speaking in an interview on Wednesday on the sidelines of the International Monetary Fund and World Bank Group spring meetings in Washington, Edun said rising crude production was positive for Nigeria's revenue, ŌĆŗfor foreign exchange and for the fiscal situation of the OPEC member.
"It gives us that extra fiscal space ŌĆŗwithin which to look at ... helping the vulnerable households at this time."
However, support would be targeted, ŌüĀsaid Edun, adding "there is no thought of any return or retardation to broad untargeted subsidies."
Edun said the country suffered ŌĆŗno fuel shortages thanks to the Dangote refinery.
"We have managed to keep the pumps going and adequate supply of petroleum products, ŌĆŗparticularly petrol and diesel and so forth, and even jet fuel."
President Bola Tinubu since 2023 has rolled out Nigeria's most ambitious economic overhaul in decades by ending costly fuel and energy subsidies, devaluing the currency and changing the tax system which - together with rising oil ŌĆŗoutput - has supported economic growth of around 4%.
But inflation in Africa's most populous nation is still high, interest rates ŌĆŗelevated and fiscal space tight as the government struggles with heavy debt-service costs, weak non-oil revenues and continued reliance on crude exports.
Data out ŌĆīon ŌüĀWednesday showed consumer inflation quickened for the first time in a year in March - a headache for Tinubu's government ahead of elections scheduled for January next year.
Asked about the impact on the economy from the Iran war which is expected to crimp growth around the globe, Edun said the outlook was very uncertain.
"The impact on growth can be ... either minimal, but over time ŌĆŗit could, if things drag ŌĆŗon, it could lead to ŌüĀworld recession," he said.
The IMF trimmed its GDP growth forecast for Nigeria to 4.1% for this year from a January prediction of 4.4%, though it expects growth to accelerate ŌĆŗto 4.3% in 2027. The Fund said that growth momentum benefited from improved macroeconomic stability ŌĆŗand positive terms-of-trade ŌüĀeffects, though warned of headwinds from higher goods and transport costs.
Meanwhile the government was committed to continuing its reform programme, said Edun.
"Nigeria is in a position where the resilience that has been built in the economy is actually very obvious for ŌüĀall to ŌĆŗsee," said Edun.
"We are very much focused on maintaining the reform pathway ŌĆŗand very much focused on improving the resilience of the Nigerian economy to withstand external shocks."
Asked about its relationship with the Fund, Edun said "Nigeria ŌĆŗis not looking for any financial support from the IMF."

item.title: Pakistan weighs spot LNG as Iran war hits supply, ramps up domestic oil and gas
item.description:
ISLAMABAD, April 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan is considering buying ŌĆīliquefied natural gas (LNG) on the spot market to offset supply disruptions caused by the Iran war but would favour government-to-government deals to avoid having to pay steep premiums, its petroleum minister said.
Qatar's force majeure has forced ŌĆŗPakistan to make costly spot purchases or find alternative fuels ahead of summer ŌĆŗdemand.
Spot LNG cargoes have surged to $20 to $30 per mmBtu amid the Middle East ŌüĀconflict, Ali Pervaiz Malik, the petroleum minister told Reuters on Wednesday.
Purchases would depend on whether ŌĆŗprices are acceptable to the power sector, including under existing government-to-government arrangements with Azerbaijan's SOCAR.
Pakistan has ŌĆŗalso been routing some crude supplies via Saudi Arabia's Red Sea port of Yanbu to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, with Malik saying insurance costs on that route were lower than routes crossing or near Hormuz.
Pakistan ŌĆŗimports nearly all of its oil, much of it via the Strait of Hormuz, and ŌĆŗremains exposed to supply shocks despite cutting its LNG reliance in recent years as gas is still needed ŌĆīto ŌüĀmeet the country's peak summer power demand.
It has begun commercial output from its highest-ever producing oil and gas well, as it shores up domestic supply.
"We have arrangements in place to meet domestic and industrial requirements," Malik said, adding that gas disruptions have not led to major curbs, ŌĆŗwith eight of 10 ŌĆŗfertiliser plants operating.
Officials are ŌüĀalso considering the use of costlier fuels such as furnace oil to limit load shedding, although at the expense of higher tariffs. Malik ŌĆŗwarned that prolonged shortages could threaten food security.
Pakistan's Baragzai X-01 well in ŌĆŗnorthwestern Khyber ŌüĀPakhtunkhwa is producing about 15,000 barrels of oil per day and 45 million cubic feet of gas, with output expected to rise further, its state-run operator Oil and Gas Development Company Ltd (OGDC) (OGDC.PSX) , opens new tab ŌüĀsaid.
The well ŌĆŗcould reach up to 25,000 bpd and 60 mmcfd ŌĆŗof gas, making it Pakistan's highest-producing well, and may contribute around 10% of crude output while cutting the country's import ŌĆŗbill by about $329 million annually, OGDC said.

item.title: US may impose secondary sanctions on Iran oil purchases
item.description: WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - U.S. ŌĆīTreasury ŌĆŗSecretary ŌĆŗScott ŌüĀBessent ŌĆŗon ŌĆŗWednesday said ŌĆŗthe ŌĆŗUnited States ŌĆīcould ŌüĀimpose ŌĆŗsecondary ŌĆŗsanctions ŌüĀon ŌĆŗcountries ŌĆŗthat ŌüĀpurchase ŌüĀIranian ŌĆŗoil.

item.title: White House offers no hint of Iran war cost as it seeks military funding surge
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - White House budget director Russell Vought said on Wednesday he could not estimate the cost of the Iran war, as he defended President ŌĆŗDonald Trump's request for a massive $1.5 trillion annual military budget against bipartisan criticism from U.S. lawmakers who cited the Pentagon's historic lack ŌĆīof financial accountability.
"We're not ready to come to you with a request. We're still working on it. We're working through to figure out what's needed," Vought told a hearing of the House of Representatives Budget Committee. "I don't have a ballpark."
The cost of the war with Iran, which Trump began alongside Israel on February 28, has remained an open question on Capitol ŌĆŗHill. An initial $200 billion request for additional funding for the war met with stiff opposition in Congress last month.
Vought appeared before the panel to ŌĆŗdiscuss Trump's proposed budget for fiscal year 2027, with its $500 billion increase in military spending and 10% reduction for non-defense programs.
The ŌüĀrequest is intended to reflect Republican priorities heading into the November midterm elections, in which Trump's Republicans hope to retain control over the House of Representatives ŌĆŗand the Senate but face growing public concern about the cost of living, energy prices and the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran .
Democrats took issue with Vought's ŌĆŗassertions that healthcare, education and low-income energy assistance programs were marred by fraud.
"I'm so glad you asked about fraud, because you are coming back to ask for a $1.5 trillion budget for the Department of Defense," Democratic Representative Pramila Jayapal of Washington state told the budget director. "The Department of Defense is the only federal agency that has never passed an audit ... But ŌĆŗyou're not going after any of that."
Vought said the administration is pursuing "inefficiencies" at the Pentagon.
"I don't think you're doing enough," said Republican Representative Glenn Grothman, who ŌĆŗcalled for a Pentagon audit to be completed before Congress votes on defense spending.
"There is so much arrogance in that agency," added Grothman, of Wisconsin. "They just say we don't have ŌĆīto do ŌüĀit on audit. We're so damn important. We don't care what Congress thinks."
Vought promoted Trump's budget proposal for the fiscal year beginning October 1 as aimed at reducing spending. He promoted Trump's 2025 tax-cut-and-spending package known as the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act" as an initiative that achieved $2 trillion in mandatory savings through cuts to Medicaid health coverage and food assistance to low-income families.
That bill, which extended 2017 tax cuts, will add $4.7 trillion to U.S. deficits over the next decade, while reduced ŌĆŗimmigration will add another $500 billion, according to ŌĆŗthe nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office.
Representative Brendan Boyle of Pennsylvania, the budget panel's top Democrat, pointed to forecasts saying the legislation's healthcare cuts would mean the loss of health coverage for more than 15 million people. Vought said they were able-bodied adults, people in the country ŌĆŗillegally or ineligible for benefits.
"You're going to sit here with a straight face and say they're all illegals? They ŌĆŗwere all defrauding ŌüĀthe system? That's actually your position?" Boyle asked.
"Yes," Vought replied.
Democratic Representative Scott Peters of California pointed out to Vought that the watchdog Government Accountability Office has found the administration illegally withheld billions of dollars allocated for National Institutes of Health grants, public schools and Head Start early education programs nationwide.
"Do you dispute GAO's findings?" Peters asked.
"Yes. GAO ŌüĀis typically ŌĆŗwrong. They're very partisan," Vought replied.
To become law, Trump's proposed budget needs approval from Congress ŌĆŗat a time when Republicans are trying to overcome Democratic opposition to funding for Trump's immigration crackdown , just months after the longest government shutdown in U.S. history . Democrats have already declared the budget proposal dead ŌĆŗon arrival, leaving government funding to closed-door negotiations between appropriators.

item.title: Ukraine says it is employing new integrated drone-infantry warfare system
item.description:
April 15 (Reuters) - Ukraine's Defence Ministry said on Wednesday it was introducing a ŌĆŗnew model of combat operations integrating ŌĆīdrone warfare with infantry and pointed to successes it said had been ŌĆŗachieved in retaking territory from ŌĆŗRussian forces in the south of ŌüĀthe country.
"A new model of warfare ŌĆŗis being introduced, drone-assault units that ŌĆŗcombine aerial and ground unmanned systems with infantry into a single integrated system," the ŌĆŗministry said in a statement ŌĆŗon Telegram.
"This approach has already produced results ŌĆīin ŌüĀthe south, where since February a large area of territory has been liberated, precisely thanks to the use ŌĆŗof ŌĆŗthese advanced ŌüĀunits."
Ukraine's top military commander, Oleksandr Syrskyi, said on Wednesday ŌĆŗthat Kyiv's forces had regained ŌĆŗcontrol ŌüĀof nearly 50 sq km (19 sq miles) of its territory from Russia ŌüĀin ŌĆŗMarch, building on its ŌĆŗgains since the start of the year.

item.title: US envoy Waltz appears to torpedo Bachelet as next UN leader
item.description:
WASHINGTON, April 15 (Reuters) - The U.S. envoy to the United Nations, Mike Waltz, appeared to torpedo the ŌĆŗcandidacy of former Chilean President Michelle Bachelet to be the next leader of the world body on ŌĆīWednesday by saying he shared a U.S. senator's concerns about her fitness for the job.
At a U.S. Senate committee hearing, Republican Senator Pete Ricketts of Nebraska charged that Bachelet had pulled punches as U.N. human rights chief in a 2022 report in failing to label China's ŌĆŗactions against Uyghur Muslims as genocide, and has also promoted abortion as a fundamental human right.
Waltz said he ŌĆŗwas not currently in a position to say whom the United States would support to ŌüĀreplace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, and whom it would oppose. But he said in response to Ricketts, "I share your concerns."
He ŌĆŗadded that he was sure that U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio also shared the concerns.
A new United Nations secretary-general will ŌĆŗbe elected this year for a five-year term starting on January 1, 2027.
Support of the five permanent veto-wielding members of the U.N. Security Council - the United States, Russia, China, Britain and France - is vital for a candidate to succeed.
Bachelet also fell foul of China over the ŌĆŗUyghur report, which said the detention of Uyghurs and other Muslims in the country's Xinjiang province may constitute crimes ŌĆŗagainst humanity.
A representative for Bachelet's campaign did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Waltz said the conventional wisdom and in New York, where ŌĆīthe ŌüĀUnited Nations has its headquarters, was that because there had never been a female secretary general, or one from Latin America, that the future U.N. leader should therefore be a Latin American woman.
"We have taken the position of we just need the best," he said. "And this institution desperately needs strong, effective leadership."
Waltz said, "reform, reform, reform ... and getting back to basics ŌĆŗon peace and security will be ŌĆŗat the top of ŌüĀour criteria."
So far four candidates have been nominated, including Bachelet, Chile's first female head of state, who twice served as president of the South American nation.
In March, Chile withdrew its ŌĆŗbacking for her candidacy after a change in leadership in the country and a ŌĆŗsharp shift to ŌüĀthe right, but she said she would press ahead with support from Brazil and Mexico.
Bachelet was U.N. high commissioner for human rights from 2018-22 and executive director of U.N. Women from 2010-13.
The other declared candidates are Rafael Grossi, a veteran Argentine diplomat ŌüĀwho is ŌĆŗcurrently director-general of the International Atomic Energy Agency, Rebeca Grynspan, a former vice ŌĆŗpresident of Costa Rica, and Macky Sall, the former president of Senegal.
The candidates are due to take part in interactive dialogues , opens new tab next week at the United ŌĆŗNations that will be broadcast live, starting with Bachelet on Tuesday.

item.title: Edison says Qatar may extend gas force majeure, sees US LNG filling gap
item.description:
MILAN, April 15 (Reuters) - Qatar may extend its force majeure on gas supplies beyond mid-June, Italian importer Edison ŌĆŗsaid on Wednesday, while predicting the gap will be plugged by ŌĆīU.S. supply instead of Russian gas.
Speaking in a media briefing, Edison (EDNn.MI) , opens new tab CEO Nicola Monti and its head of gas portfolio management Fabio Dubini said the Italian group bought seven cargoes of liquefied ŌĆŗnatural gas from the United States after 10 were cancelled by QatarEnergy ŌĆŗbetween April and mid-June due to the Middle East war.
Edison has a ŌüĀlongŌĆæterm contract with QatarEnergy to receive 6.4 billion cubic metres of LNG per ŌĆŗyear, around 10% of Italy's annual consumption.
The utility, Italy's second-biggest gas importer, last month ŌĆŗreceived notification from its Gulf supplier that it would not deliver 10 cargoes - equal to 1.4 billion cubic metres of gas - from April to mid-June. Attacks by Iran knocked out 17% of QatarEnergy's ŌĆŗLNG export capacity last month, according to its CEO.
QatarEnergy could not be immediately reached ŌĆŗoutside normal business hours.
The deliveries to Edison's final customers in Italy are not at risk, Edison ŌĆīsaid, ŌüĀeven if Dubini said it was reasonable to expect an extension of the disruption of QatarEnergy's supply.
"I think the market at the moment, while tight, offers sufficient flexibility options to allow us to continue getting supplies from other areas," Monti said, when ŌĆŗasked whether Europe should ŌĆŗreconsider its planned ŌüĀban on Russian LNG imports, as Italian energy group Eni has said.
Monti cited the startup this year of two U.S. LNG ŌĆŗplants ŌĆö Venture Global's Plaquemines and Golden Pass, which is partly ŌĆŗowned by ŌüĀQatarEnergy and Exxon Mobil ŌĆö as a reason for confidence in supply. Other plants are also under construction, he said.
"Over the next 18 months, the market will return to being structurally ŌüĀbalanced," Monti ŌĆŗsaid.
A previously expected LNG supply glut around 2027 ŌĆŗto 2028 is likely to shift to 2029 to 2030 due to the temporary shortfall of gas from ŌĆŗthe Middle East, Monti said.

item.title: 'Significant' fire breaks out at Viva Energy's refinery in Australia
item.description:
April 15 (Reuters) - Firefighters have ŌĆŗarrived on the scene to respond to a ŌĆ£significantŌĆØ ŌĆīfire at Viva Energy Group's Geelong refinery in southern Australia, according to local fire authorities.
The extent of the damage was not immediately clear. ŌĆŗThe blaze was reported at around 11 p.m. ŌĆŗlocal time on Wednesday, Fire Rescue Victoria said in ŌüĀa statement, adding it was not yet under control ŌĆŗbut all staff had been accounted for.
The dispatch to the ŌĆŗrefinery in Corio in the state of Victoria came after multiple calls reporting explosions and flames, it said.
The refinery can process up to ŌĆŗ120,000 barrels of oil per day, according to the company's ŌĆŗwebsite.
It is one of two refineries remaining in Australia and employs over ŌĆī1,100 ŌüĀpeople, supplying over 50 percent of VictoriaŌĆÖs and 10 percent of AustraliaŌĆÖs fuel, the website said.
Viva Energy Group did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.
Australia has faced ŌĆŗfuel security ŌĆŗconcerns since Iran ŌüĀall but closed the Strait of Hormuz after being attacked by the U.S. and ŌĆŗIsrael.
Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said last month ŌĆŗthe ŌüĀgovernment would halve the excise on fuel and diesel and remove the heavy road user charge for three months to ŌüĀhelp ŌĆŗhouseholds cope with a surge in ŌĆŗcosts driven by the Iran war.

item.title: World Bank launches 'Water Forward' programme to tackle global water stress
item.description:
LONDON, April 15 (Reuters) - The World Bank and other top development lenders launched a new global initiative dubbed Water Forward on Wednesday, aimed at improving secure water ŌĆŗaccess for a billion people within the next four years.
The programme seeks ŌĆīto boost investment in water management while encouraging governments to treat water as a strategic economic resource rather than a low-cost public utility. It will focus on mobilising private capital ŌĆŗand philanthropic money alongside public funding, the World Bank said.
"Water is foundational ŌĆŗto how economies function," the head of the World Bank, ŌüĀAjay Banga, said in a statement, adding that the task was now to "deliver ŌĆŗreliable water services at scale."
Global demand for freshwater is expected to outstrip supply by ŌĆŗup to 40% by the end of the decade, the World Bank estimates, with water-related shocks already costing some countries several percentage points of annual economic growth.
Climate change is intensifying ŌĆŗboth droughts and floods, placing pressure on public finances and vulnerable communities, particularly ŌĆŗin fast-growing cities. A report last year estimated that over 2.1 billion people lack safe drinking ŌĆīwater, ŌüĀand more than 3.4 billion live without adequate sanitation.
Water Forward will initially focus on 14 countries in water-stressed regions in Africa, the Middle East and South Asia and prioritise projects that reduce leakage in urban areas, modernise irrigation, improve wastewater ŌĆŗreuse and expand ŌĆŗdata-driven planning.
Other development ŌüĀbanks involved include the European Investment Bank, Asian Development Bank, the Inter-American Development Bank and the New Development Bank. The ŌĆŗlatter institution was established by BRICS nations Brazil, Russia, India, ŌĆŗChina and ŌüĀSouth Africa.
The World Bank said it estimated that 4 billion people experience water scarcity due to a mix of unclear government policies, weak regulations and financially unsustainable utilities.
Its ŌüĀcommitment ŌĆŗwas to deliver water security to 400 million ŌĆŗpeople by 2030, it added, while "additional partner commitments" would take the Water Forward programme's total reach to ŌĆŗmore than 1 billion people.

item.title: Trading Day: S&P 500, Nasdaq nab all-time closing highs, buoyed by Middle East optimism
item.description:
NEW YORK, April 15 (Reuters) - The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both logged record closing highs on Wednesday as investors assessed developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict and turned their focus to corporate earnings.
And before you read on, don't forget to sign up , opens new tab for Jamie McGeever's upcoming LSEG webinar ŌĆŗon April 23, where he'll talk about safe havens in uncertain times with his ROI colleague Mike Dolan.
Here are a ŌĆīfew articles I recommend to help you make sense of what happened in markets today.
Today's Key Market Moves
Today's Talking Points
* S&P 500, Nasdaq reached their first record closing highs since the U.S.-Iran conflict began
Hopes of de-escalation and robust earnings expectations pushed ŌĆīthe indexes ŌüĀto all-time closing highs, reassuring investors that a bull market remains intact.
Reaching those records during an active geopolitical crisis signified a shift in market positioning as traders appeared more willing to price in less severe escalation risks in the near term.
* Shares of Allbirds soared 582.3% after announcing a move from footwear to AI computing infrastructure
In an unusual pivot, the San Francisco-based company said it would execute a $50 million convertible financing agreement with an ŌĆŗinstitutional investor, the proceeds of which ŌĆŗwill be used to acquire ŌüĀgraphics processing units (GPUs).
* Trump threatens to fire Powell if he doesn't quit Fed board
Trump has threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell from his separate seat on the central bank's Board of Governors if he does ŌĆŗnot relinquish the post when his term as Fed chief ends on May 15.
The administration's threats against ŌĆŗPowell, including an ongoing ŌüĀcriminal investigation, could delay Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh, Trump's pick to succeed Powell.
What could move markets tomorrow?
